mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, discount-rate pressure on equities eases and the S&P 500 tends to post positive 20-day forward returns. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with higher near-term returns than rate-hold or rate-rise periods.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window but SPX 20d forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of cases, or the conditional mean return is not above the unconditional mean, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which should push the S&P 500 lower over the next month. The effect is strongest after the unemployment rate has already accelerated, because equity markets then begin to price a sharper earnings slowdown.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, during UNRATE acceleration episodes, SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases or the average return is not negative relative to non-trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal demand and supports revenue expectations, so accelerating sales should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The relationship fails if sales strength is driven by inflation rather than real demand and does not lift equity performance.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: If above-trend RSAFS readings are followed by negative or flat SPX 20d returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses sign consistency over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in INDPRO and HOUST indicates improving real activity and construction-led supply momentum, which should support cyclicals and lift the S&P 500 over the next quarter. This is a joint mechanism because industrial production alone is weaker when housing is contracting, and vice versa.
failure_condition: Reject if positive joint moves in INDPRO and HOUST fail to produce positive SPX 60d forward returns more often than not, or if either series alone explains the sign better than the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightness increases and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should fail when inflation is falling or policy is clearly easing because real-rate pressure then recedes.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes do not correspond to negative SPX 20d returns in at least 60% of observations, or if the interaction term is not negative after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below recent norms signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and construction activity, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is a short-horizon risk-off signal because housing is an early cyclical indicator for consumption and credit demand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by at least 5%
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend HOUST episodes are not followed by negative SPX 5d returns more often than positive returns, or if the average 5d return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market slowdown, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month by lowering earnings expectations and consumption outlook. This is a thresholded labor stress signal and fails when employment weakness is not accompanied by rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls over 3 months and UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-stress episodes do not produce negative SPX 20d forward returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS and UNRATE are used jointly.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-hike stabilization regime, stronger GDPC1 growth supports earnings and risk appetite, so positive revisions in real GDP should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The relationship fails in recessionary contraction phases where GDP growth is already collapsing and markets trade on downturn risk rather than growth acceleration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth turns positive on the latest release
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP growth turns are not followed by positive SPX 60d returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal is negative during stabilization episodes across multiple rolling windows.
